title: Python for AI: Zero to Project

modules.title: Why Python and Why Now: The AI Developer's On-Ramp
modules.sub: Understanding why Python is the entry point to AI work
modules.description: Learners explore why Python became the dominant language in AI development and what that means for someone starting today. They map out the landscape of tools, jobs, and projects that Python unlocks, building a clear picture of where this skill leads.

modules.title: Python Basics That Actually Matter for AI Work
modules.sub: Learning only the Python concepts AI projects actually use
modules.description: Rather than covering every Python feature, learners focus on the specific concepts — variables, loops, functions, and data structures — that show up repeatedly in AI scripts. They write small, working code snippets and begin to see how these building blocks connect to real tasks.

modules.title: Working With Data: Files, APIs, and Inputs
modules.sub: Reading, handling, and moving data through your code
modules.description: Learners practice reading files, processing user inputs, and making basic API calls — the three data flows that appear in almost every AI project. By the end of the module they can write a script that takes in information, does something with it, and returns a useful output.

modules.title: Your First AI Integration: Calling a Model From Your Own Code
modules.sub: Connecting a live AI model directly to your Python script
modules.description: Learners write Python code that sends a prompt to a real AI API and handles the response — making the jump from studying Python to actually using it with AI. They experiment with different inputs and outputs, building intuition for how AI models behave when called programmatically.

modules.title: Building a Real Project: Design, Build, and Ship
modules.sub: Designing and completing a shareable AI-powered project
modules.description: Learners choose a project idea, plan its structure, and build it from scratch using the skills developed in previous modules. The focus is on finishing — making decisions, solving blockers, and arriving at something complete enough to share and explain to others.

modules.title: Reading and Fixing AI-Generated Code Confidently
modules.sub: Understanding, testing, and correcting code you didn't write
modules.description: Learners work through examples of AI-generated code, practicing how to read unfamiliar scripts, spot errors, and make targeted fixes without needing to understand every line. This module builds the confidence to use AI coding tools as a collaborator rather than a black box.

modules.title: Sharing Your Work: GitHub, Portfolios, and Getting Noticed
modules.sub: Putting your project online where the right people can find it
modules.description: Learners publish their completed project to GitHub, write a clear README, and connect it to a simple portfolio presence. They learn what recruiters, collaborators, and communities actually look for when evaluating someone's technical work online.

modules.title: What to Learn Next and How to Keep Momentum
modules.sub: Mapping a personalized path forward after your first project
modules.description: Learners assess what they've built, identify which directions — data science, web apps, automation, or deeper AI work — align with their interests, and choose specific next steps. The module closes with a framework for staying consistent and continuing to grow without a formal curriculum to follow.

synopsis: Python is the language of AI, and you don't need a CS degree to get started â you need the right path and a real project to finish. This course skips the filler and gets you to something you built yourself using Python and a live AI API.
rationale: Python remains the foundational skill for AI work, yet most intro courses don't connect Python basics to AI applications in ways that feel immediately relevant to young adults building portfolios or freelance skills.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can write Python scripts that interact with AI APIs and have a completed, shareable project that demonstrates real technical capability.
source_signals: Machine Learning 101 slidedeck: 2 years of headbanging, so you don't have to
nearest_existing: Code Is Now the Easiest Part
